BACKGROUND

A The parties intend to execute this agreement as a deed.

2 Provision and Use of Confidential Information

2.

1 The Disclosers may (but are not obliged to) disclose during the term of this Agreement, and/or may have already disclosed, Confidential Information to the Recipient for the Purpose and, in consideration of the Disclosers disclosing any Confidential Information to the Recipient and the payment to the Recipient of £1 (one pound) the receipt of which is acknowledged by the Recipient),

b020e02d-222e-43f8-9a82-346d88a90501.label: text_chunk
b020e02d-222e-43f8-9a82-346d88a90501.text: the Recipient undertakes to the Disclosers that it shall, and shall procure that its Authorised Persons shall:

5471ed68-af73-4ca6-b6b5-da590f51341e.text: 3 create and maintain the best industry standards of security (including any reasonable arrangements specified by the Disclosers from time to time and the most secure arrangements that the Recipient maintains for its own trade secrets) in order to ensure that the Confidential Information is secure from unauthorised access, and shall inform immediately the Disclosers if the Recipient becomes aware of any apparent unauthorised access.2.

f93ad2a3-b60d-4daf-9048-b0f3abff5710.label: text_chunk
f93ad2a3-b60d-4daf-9048-b0f3abff5710.text: 2 To the extent determined from time to time by the Disclosers, each of those of the Disclosers’ Group Members, if any, whose information is contained in the Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient under this Agreement shall be entitled to enforce the rights expressed in favour of the Disclosers under this Agreement in respect only of that Confidential Information belonging to it as if such rights were expressed to be in its favour.2.

ddab88f3-83c2-4dc2-9e35-5022c13eeda5.label: text_chunk
ddab88f3-83c2-4dc2-9e35-5022c13eeda5.text: 3 None of the Disclosers, its Group Members, and its or their directors, officers, agents, employees, consultants, subcontractors, or advisers makes any representation or warranty concerning the accuracy, efficacy, completeness or capabilities of the Confidential Information disclosed by it (or of any materials or media by which it is supplied), except to the extent, if any, expressly agreed by the Disclosers in writing. No representation or warranty is made that the Confidential Information shall remain unchanged.

6aa54ca3-82a7-48a1-ae19-8b4307caf42f.label: text_chunk
6aa54ca3-82a7-48a1-ae19-8b4307caf42f.text: There is no obligation on the part of the Disclosers to update or correct any inaccuracies in any Confidential Information. Any projected results or management accounts which may be contained in the Confidential Information are for indicative purposes only and are not warranted for accuracy or completeness. The Recipient must make its own independent assessment of the Confidential Information and the Purpose and rely on its own judgment in reaching any conclusion or decision.2.

7d6e4e90-1aba-4f95-9bc5-f268cfe057da.label: text_chunk
7d6e4e90-1aba-4f95-9bc5-f268cfe057da.text:
4 There is no representation, warranty or undertaking by the Disclosers to continue to disclose any Confidential Information or continue negotiations or enter into a further agreement
in relation to the Purpose or the Confidential Information, which agreement can only be subject to a formal written agreement being agreed and signed by the parties.2.5 The Recipient’s confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall survive and subsist indefinitely in relation to any Confidential Information (notwithstanding the prior termination or expiry of this Agreement).

db2bb25d-eedf-475c-a22e-6d2938b17feb.label: text_chunk
db2bb25d-eedf-475c-a22e-6d2938b17feb.text:
3 Authorised disclosures

3.1 The Recipient may disclose any of the Confidential Information to any of its Authorised Persons, provided that it informs them beforehand of the duties of confidence under this Agreement, ensures that they undertake to the Recipient to comply with the same duties of confidence, keeps a written account of each of the disclosures, advises the Disclosers immediately it becomes aware of any breach by an Authorised Person,

5b153133-7f5c-4155-aed3-9e90c8b97103.label: text_chunk
5b153133-7f5c-4155-aed3-9e90c8b97103.text:
and gives upon any request by the Disclosers evidence of compliance with this clause.3.

2 The Recipient further undertakes to procure that all Authorised Persons to whom it discloses the Confidential Information shall comply with the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement and, if required by the Disclosers, to procure that they enter into a confidentiality agreement directly with the Disclosers in reasonably similar terms as those set out under this Agreement.

62824600-1dac-4622-90cb-d3f894e832d1.label: text_chunk
62824600-1dac-4622-90cb-d3f894e832d1.text:
4 Excluded information

4.1 The Recipient's obligations under this Agreement do not apply to, and the term Confidential Information does not include, any information to the extent to which the Recipient can prove to the Disclosers’ reasonable satisfaction has been agreed by the Disclosers in writing as being excluded from Confidential Information.4.

a413592f-1e13-4fc1-8dc2-45ee328dd053.label: text_chunk
a413592f-1e13-4fc1-8dc2-45ee328dd053.text: 2 The Recipient shall not be in breach of its obligations under this Agreement to the extent that any Confidential Information received by it may be required by law or regulation having force of law; or the rules of any court or other body of competent jurisdiction; or any governmental body to be disclosed, provided in each case the Recipient, to the extent permitted by the foregoing requirement,

eeba6115-daf1-4761-a55f-5999f2da6222.label: text_chunk
eeba6115-daf1-4761-a55f-5999f2da6222.text:
immediately notifies the Disclosers in writing of any request or requirement for disclosure and of all relevant surrounding circumstances prior to disclosure and takes into account any representations made by the Disclosers in relation to the disclosure.

 The Recipient shall use its best endeavours to resist any requirement for disclosure and to assist the Disclosers in resisting that requirement.

79a1a484-0921-4044-82d6-b4a8a0cb7106.label: text_chunk
79a1a484-0921-4044-82d6-b4a8a0cb7106.text:
5 Intellectual Property Rights

The Recipient acknowledges that ownership of the Confidential Information including all Intellectual Property Rights in the Confidential Information remains vested in and shall vest in the Disclosers and its licensors, and that no licence or right is granted other than to the extent expressly set out in this Agreement.

6 Indemnity

The Recipient shall indemnify the Disclosers from and against all and any losses, damages (including any consequential, direct, indirect, special,

d64ad62a-0a89-468d-9863-4013081c2cb9.label: text_chunk
d64ad62a-0a89-468d-9863-4013081c2cb9.text: incidental or punitive damages or loss or any other form of economic loss), liability, costs (including legal fees), expenses, claims (including any settlements of claims before or after issue of proceedings), actions, proceedings, judgment sums (including sums arising from consent orders or judgments) fines and penalties, however arising out of, or in connection with, the breach of this Agreement or the unauthorised disclosure or Use of any of the Confidential Information by the Recipient or by any of its Authorised Persons.

f78407e6-30fa-43ca-9b5d-67bd24e5345b.label: text_chunk
f78407e6-30fa-43ca-9b5d-67bd24e5345b.text:
7 Equitable Relief

The Recipient recognises that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement may cause the Disclosers irreparable harm for which damages may not be an adequate remedy. Accordingly, in addition to any other remedies and damages available to the Disclosers, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Disclosers is entitled to the remedies of specific performance, injunction and other equitable relief without proof of special damages.

ef351578-8902-40be-aed1-ca837bd61b4a.label: text_chunk
ef351578-8902-40be-aed1-ca837bd61b4a.text:
8 Delivery Up

8.1 Upon receipt by the Recipient of a written demand from the Disclosers:
8.1.1 the Recipient must return or procure the return to the Disclosers or, as the Disclosers may require, destroy or procure the destruction of any and all materials containing the Confidential Information together with all copies;
8.1.

b2bc4e7f-6d26-4a47-a36c-4a984db3ec94.label: text_chunk

70a0d9da-df93-4d3e-905d-b59bc6d53c58.label: text_chunk
70a0d9da-df93-4d3e-905d-b59bc6d53c58.text:
4 the Recipient must make, and procure that the Authorised Persons shall make, no further Use of the Confidential Information.

9 Entire agreement

9.1 The parties agree that this Agreement and any documents entered into pursuant to it constitute the entire agreement between them and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and arrangements between them, whether in writing or oral in respect of its subject matter.9.

entered into pursuant to it. The Recipient shall not have any claim for innocent or negligent misrepresentation on the basis of any statement in this Agreement.

9.3 Nothing in this Agreement purports to limit or exclude any liability for fraud.

36ac62d0-8058-4b26-b9ec-99eeeadb71c3.label: text_chunk
36ac62d0-8058-4b26-b9ec-99eeeadb71c3.text:
10 Further assurance

Each party shall at the request of the other, and at the cost of the requesting party, do all acts and execute all documents which are necessary to give full effect to this Agreement.

11 Variation

No variation of this Agreement shall be valid or effective unless it is in writing, refers to this Agreement and is duly signed or executed by, or on behalf of, each party.

ef8e3f8f-2be7-4af1-9d88-1ea1af4c13b5.label: text_chunk
ef8e3f8f-2be7-4af1-9d88-1ea1af4c13b5.text:
12 Assignment and transfer of rights

The Disclosers can, but the Recipient shall not (without the Disclosers’ prior written consent), assign, novate, hold on trust, subcontract or encumber any right or obligation under this Agreement, in whole or in part.

13 Cumulative rights

The rights and remedies provided for in this Agreement are cumulative with, and not exclusive of, any rights or remedies otherwise provided by law or in equity.

371c8b76-ee89-47dd-9672-f40ed817326f.label: text_chunk
371c8b76-ee89-47dd-9672-f40ed817326f.text:
No exercise by a party of any one right or remedy shall operate so as to hinder or prevent the exercise by it of any other right or remedy.

14 Set off

The Recipient shall pay all sums that it owes to the Disclosers under this Agreement without any set-off, counterclaim, deduction or withholding of any kind, save as may be required by law.

0f0cc45b-6fe7-4c55-93b1-835d1217ae92.label: text_chunk
0f0cc45b-6fe7-4c55-93b1-835d1217ae92.text:
15 No partnership or agency

The parties are independent persons and are not partners, principal and agent or employer and employee, and this Agreement does not establish any joint venture between them, other than the contractual relationship expressly provided for in it. The Recipient shall not have, nor shall represent that it has, any authority to make any commitment on the Disclosers’ behalf.

49e7ced6-d0aa-475f-96b1-f44c3ec3c3dc.label: text_chunk
49e7ced6-d0aa-475f-96b1-f44c3ec3c3dc.text:
16 Severance

16.1 If any provision of this Agreement (or part of any provision) is or becomes illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the legality, validity and enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement shall not be affected.16.

1bf85331-99b2-4924-98b7-dd914a3c2e68.label: text_chunk
1bf85331-99b2-4924-98b7-dd914a3c2e68.text: 2 If any provision of this Agreement (or part of any provision) is or becomes illegal, invalid or unenforceable but would be legal, valid and enforceable if some part of it was deleted or modified, the provision or part-provision in question shall apply with such deletions or modifications as may be necessary to make the provision legal, valid and enforceable. In the

ee107c02-01ac-4186-8389-16dee5cb0094.label: text_chunk
ee107c02-01ac-4186-8389-16dee5cb0094.text:
event of such deletion or modification, the parties shall negotiate in good faith in order to agree the terms of a mutually acceptable alternative provision.

17 Waiver

17.1 No failure, delay or omission by either party in exercising any right, power or remedy provided by law or under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of that right, power or remedy, nor shall it preclude or restrict any future exercise of that or any other right, power or remedy.17.

82d628cb-578c-4800-9b81-1145bf1f2b4d.label: text_chunk
82d628cb-578c-4800-9b81-1145bf1f2b4d.text: 2 No single or partial exercise of any right, power or remedy provided by law or under this Agreement shall prevent any future exercise of it or the exercise of any other right, power or remedy.17.3 A waiver of any term, provision, condition or breach of this Agreement shall only be effective if given in writing and signed by the waiving party, and then only in the instance and for the purpose for which it is given.

fb718298-01cf-4e21-bb77-1777f8f82944.label: text_chunk
fb718298-01cf-4e21-bb77-1777f8f82944.text:
18 Compliance with law

Each party shall comply with all laws, enactments, regulations, regulatory policies, guidelines and industry codes applicable to them and shall maintain such authorisations and all other approvals, permits and authorities as are required from time to time to perform their obligations under or in connection with this Agreement.

a0f90b58-91d1-4840-baeb-a1046284140e.label: text_chunk
a0f90b58-91d1-4840-baeb-a1046284140e.text:
19 Counterparts

19.1 This Agreement may be signed in any number of separate counterparts, each of which when signed and dated shall be an original, and such counterparts taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement.19.2 Each party may evidence their signature of this Agreement by transmitting by email a signed signature page of this Agreement in PDF format together with the final version of this Agreement in PDF or Word format, which shall constitute an original signed counterpart of this Agreement.

32d97d1b-7ea0-4573-9a2e-47e849a7d9fb.label: text_chunk
32d97d1b-7ea0-4573-9a2e-47e849a7d9fb.text:
Each party adopting this method of signing shall, following circulation by email, provide the original, hard copy signed signature page to the other parties as soon as reasonably practicable.

20 Costs and expenses

Each party shall pay its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the negotiation, preparation, signature and performance of this Agreement (and any documents referred to in it).

21 Language

21.1 The language of this Agreement is English.

929a034f-864d-44b5-bcee-d3f860556b17.label: text_chunk
929a034f-864d-44b5-bcee-d3f860556b17.text:
All documents, notices, waivers, variations and other written communications relating to this Agreement shall be in English. 21.2 If this Agreement and any document relating to it is translated, the English version shall prevail.

22 Third party rights

Except as expressly provided for in this Agreement, a person who is not a party to this Agreement shall not have any rights under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any of the provisions of this Agreement.

e741b3da-9c37-4bdd-835b-b7dd28a0a647.label: text_chunk
e741b3da-9c37-4bdd-835b-b7dd28a0a647.text:
The parties shall not, however, be required to notify or obtain the consent of any third party in order to rescind or vary this Agreement.

23 Governing law

This Agreement and any dispute or claim arising out of, or in connection with it, its subject matter or its formation (including non-contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of England and Wales.

cd40829a-f1ff-465f-a60d-ddfe61436fec.label: text_chunk
cd40829a-f1ff-465f-a60d-ddfe61436fec.text:
24 Jurisdiction

The parties irrevocably agree that the courts of England and Wales shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any dispute or claim arising out of, or in connection with, this Agreement, its subject matter or its formation (including non-contractual disputes or claims).THIS AGREEMENT IS EXECUTED AS A DEED by the parties on the date set out at the head of this deed

properties.entity_description: Intellectual Property Rights are legal rights that grant the creators of original works exclusive rights to their use and distribution, ensuring ownership and protection of their creations.

Copyright is a legal right that grants the creator of original works exclusive rights to their use and distribution, typically for a limited time, with the intention of enabling the creator to receive compensation for their intellectual investment.

Patents are exclusive rights granted for an invention, which provides the patent holder the right to exclude others from making, using, selling, or distributing the patented invention without permission for a specified period.
Trademarks are symbols, words, or phrases legally registered or established by use as representing a company or product, providing a means for consumers to identify the source of goods or services.

Trade Secrets refer to practices, designs, formulas, processes, or any information that provides a business advantage over competitors who do not know or use it, and are protected by law as long as they remain confidential.

properties.relationship_description: The rights mentioned in the text, such as unfair competition rights and rights to sue, are incorporated within the context of the Franchise Agreement.

community_summary.18:
### Summary of Relationships Involving Disclosers and Recipients

1. **Disclosers and Recipient**: The Disclosers provide confidential or commercially sensitive information to the Recipient, establishing a relationship based on trust and confidentiality. This relationship is governed by an agreement that outlines the terms of information sharing, ownership, and compliance obligations.

2. **Ownership and Control**: The Disclosers retain ownership of the Confidential Information and associated Intellectual Property Rights. They maintain control over the rights and obligations of the Recipient, who cannot transfer these rights without prior consent.

3. **Compliance and Notification**: The Disclosers require the Recipient to demonstrate compliance with confidentiality obligations and to notify them of any requests for disclosure, indicating a supervisory and communicative relationship.

4. **Affiliated Entities**: The Disclosers are associated with Group Members, indicating a shared organizational structure where information provided by the Disclosers may be utilized by these Group Members in their business activities.

5. **Entitlements and Remedies**: The Disclosers are entitled to specific remedies as outlined in the agreement in case of breaches by the Recipient, reinforcing the importance of adherence to the established terms.

Overall, the relationships highlight a structured framework of information sharing, ownership, compliance, and accountability between the Disclosers and the Recipient, with implications for affiliated entities within the same organizational context.
community_summary.19:
### Summary of Relationships Involving the Recipient and Disclosers

1. **Information Transfer**: The Recipient is set to receive Confidential Information from the Disclosers, establishing a direct dependency where the Recipient relies on the Disclosers for critical information under a legal agreement.

2. **Compliance and Responsibility**: The Recipient is responsible for ensuring that its Authorised Persons comply with the terms set by the Disclosers regarding the handling of confidential information. This includes oversight of their actions, as any unauthorized disclosure by them can lead to liability for the Recipient.

3. **Obligations and Duties**: The Recipient has several obligations towards the Disclosers:
   - To inform the Disclosers of any unauthorized access to the confidential information.
   - To assist the Disclosers in resisting any disclosure requirements, highlighting a collaborative effort to protect confidentiality.
   - To return or destroy materials containing Confidential Information upon written demand from the Disclosers.
   - To delete Confidential Information as required by the Disclosers, demonstrating a duty of care.
   - To pay all sums owed to the Disclosers under the terms of the Agreement, indicating a financial relationship.

4. **Acknowledgment of Terms**: The Recipient acknowledges the terms and conditions set forth in the Agreement, which defines its confidentiality obligations and the potential harm that breaches may cause to the Disclosers.

5. **Independence**: The Recipient does not have the authority to make commitments on behalf of the Disclosers, establishing a clear boundary of independence between the two parties.

Overall, the relationships highlight a structured framework of information sharing, compliance, and mutual obligations, with the Recipient playing a crucial role in safeguarding the confidentiality of the information provided by the Disclosers.

The summary of the relationships surrounding the Agreement is as follows:

1. **Parties Involved**: The Agreement involves one or more parties who are bound by its terms and conditions, establishing a legal relationship among them.

2. **Confidential Information**: The Agreement governs the use and protection of Confidential Information, outlining the legal framework for how such information should be handled by the parties.

3. **Binding Obligations**: The Agreement serves as a binding document for the Recipient, detailing the legal obligations and responsibilities that must be adhered to.

4. **Execution Requirement**: Each party must execute the Agreement for it to be valid, highlighting the necessity of mutual consent and commitment.

5. **Third-Party Rights**: The Agreement acknowledges the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999, indicating that it recognizes the rights of third parties in relation to its provisions.

6. **Governing Law**: The Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales, meaning that any legal interpretations or disputes will be resolved under this jurisdiction.

7. **Exclusive Jurisdiction**: The Agreement specifies that the courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes arising from it, reinforcing the legal relationship between the document and the geographical location.

Overall, the Agreement establishes a comprehensive legal framework that binds the involved parties, protects confidential information, recognizes third-party rights, and delineates the governing laws and jurisdiction for any potential disputes.

2. **HRM and SAP Services**: HRM receives SAP Services as part of a business transaction facilitated by a Disclosing Party, indicating a transactional relationship where HRM benefits from these services.

3. **HRM and the Province**: HRM acknowledges that the Province has the authority to disclose confidential information to its external or internal auditors. This relationship highlights the Province's role in managing and sharing information related to HRM.

Overall, the relationships illustrate the complexities of confidentiality and information exchange in business operations, as well as the implications of contractual agreements like NDAs.

In summary, the Province acts as a facilitator in the provision of SAP services, coordinating between the SAP Service Provider and the HRM to ensure effective service delivery.

The relationships outlined highlight the City’s proactive approach to managing infrastructure and securing funding for various projects. 

1. **Funding Relationships**: The City receives financial support from Measure W, which is utilized to enhance its projects. Additionally, the City actively applies for funding from the State, particularly through programs like the State Revolving Fund, to bolster its financial resources.

2. **Project Management**: The City is responsible for managing the Marie Canyon Green Streets project, indicating its role in overseeing urban development initiatives as part of its Capital Improvement Program. Furthermore, the City oversees the City Hall Roof Replacement project, showcasing its commitment to maintaining local infrastructure.

3. **Collaboration for Improvement**: The City collaborates with FEMA to develop a storm drain inventory, leveraging federal funding to improve infrastructure in response to challenges posed by events such as the Woolsey Fire.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City’s strategic efforts in funding acquisition, project management, and collaboration with federal agencies to enhance community infrastructure and resilience.

The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure** and **Los Angeles County Flood Control**: The Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure project is dependent on obtaining a maintenance agreement from the Los Angeles County Flood Control organization, which is essential for the project's construction to move forward.

2. **Regulation 070** and **vehicle impact protection devices**: Regulation 070 stipulates the necessity for installing vehicle impact protection devices. This regulation aims to enhance safety in areas that are close to outdoor pedestrian seating, ensuring the protection of pedestrians in these environments. 

Overall, these relationships highlight the importance of regulatory compliance and organizational agreements in the development and safety measures of urban infrastructure projects.

Overall, the summary illustrates how specific repair and improvement projects are geographically and functionally linked, particularly focusing on the areas of Malibu Road and Birdview Avenue within the broader context of drainage improvements in Malibu Park.

3. **Evaluation of Construction Bids**: The City received and evaluated construction bids for a project but ultimately rejected them due to a budget shortfall, illustrating its active role in the procurement process and fiscal responsibility.

6. **Traffic Signals Infrastructure**: The City maintains traffic signals as part of its infrastructure, which are essential for managing vehicular flow and ensuring public safety on the roads.

Overall, these relationships demonstrate the City's proactive management of infrastructure, collaboration with key agencies, and commitment to effective urban planning and disaster recovery.

In summary, the Planning Department's adherence to CEQA regulations is essential for responsible development oversight, while the requirement for traffic signals to have a backup power system underscores the importance of continuous operation for public safety.
